methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $500M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Wandelbots, a Series G robotics and manufacturing automation company founded by Christian Piechnick, has raised $126M to date. The valuation landscape includes a limited comparable set: one public peer (277810.KQ) and eight private robotics and manufacturing companies including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics. The sector contains sufficient public and private activity, though direct same-stage comparables are scarce.

A single applicable valuation method—stage-progression analysis—was employed given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($126M) as a proxy foundation ($500M implied base), the analysis applies a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $625M. This method assumes Wandelbots will follow conventional stage progression patterns, though private frontier-tech companies often exhibit heterogeneous growth trajectories.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $531M to $719M, with a central estimate of $625M. Material uncertainties include: the absence of sufficient narrow-set peer multiples (peer-median methodology was not applicable due to insufficient same- or adjacent-stage comparables), reliance on a single method, and the company's undisclosed current valuation. Further precision would require access to recent transaction data or disclosed Series G terms from comparable robotics platforms.
